# RHDP MaaS Documentation

> RHDP MaaS is the Red Hat Demo Platform's Model-as-a-Service implementation, built on LiteMaaS — an open-source platform developed by the Red Hat AI Services BU. It provides users with managed access to AI/LLM models through virtual API keys, with usage tracking, rate limiting, and budget controls.

## What is RHDP MaaS

RHDP MaaS is RHDP's deployment and implementation of LiteMaaS. LiteMaaS is the upstream open-source software (github.com/rh-aiservices-bu/litemaas) that wraps LiteLLM (an open-source AI proxy) with a management layer: a React frontend portal, a Fastify backend API, and PostgreSQL for state. Model servers run on OpenShift AI (KServe). The RHDP-specific deployment is managed using the rhpds.litemaas Ansible collection.

Users never interact with model servers directly. RHDP MaaS sits in between — managing who can access which models, enforcing budgets and rate limits, and providing every user a simple OpenAI-compatible API endpoint without managing any infrastructure.

## Documentation

- [Architecture](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/architecture.html): System design — 5 diagrams covering the platform overview, production deployment, GitOps for models, platform installation via Ansible, and per-workshop key creation.
- [Product Overview](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/product/overview.html): What RHDP MaaS does, key features, role hierarchy, and user flow.
- [Models Reference](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/product/models.html): Model capability types (Chat, Embeddings, Document Conversion), API endpoints, curl and SDK examples.
- [Deployment Guide](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/install/deployment.html): Prerequisites, Ansible collection variables, step-by-step deployment, post-install checklist.
- [Day-2 Operations](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/day2/operations.html): Upgrading, scaling, key cleanup, admin management.
- [Guardrails](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/day2/guardrails.html): Content filtering — block PII, credentials, and harmful content using LiteLLM's built-in content filter.
- [Claude Code MCP](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/day2/mcp.html): Connect Claude Code to RHDP MaaS via MCP.
- [Grafana & Metrics](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/day2/grafana.html): Grafana dashboards for RHOAI, vLLM metrics, ServiceMonitors, GPU monitoring.
- [Monitoring](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/monitoring.html): Grafana dashboards, benchmark tool, key cleanup cronjob.
- [Troubleshooting](https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/troubleshooting/common.html): Common issues with exact fix commands.

## Key Technical Facts

- Production cluster: maas.redhatworkshops.io, namespace: maas-rhdp
- LiteMaaS version: v0.4.0 (backend + frontend)
- LiteLLM: custom fork quay.io/rh-aiservices-bu/litellm-non-root:main-v1.81.0-stable-custom
- LiteLLM: 5 replicas, Backend: 1 replica, Frontend: 3 replicas
- PostgreSQL 16 with 10Gi storage (gp3-csi)
- GCP project: rh-summit-ai-workshops (covers all Vertex AI models)
- Auth: OpenShift SSO (sso-internal + infra-ldap)
- Model servers on OpenShift AI (KServe) in llm-hosting namespace
- Deployment: rhpds.litemaas Ansible collection
- Key creation: rhpds.litellm_virtual_keys Ansible collection
- GitHub: https://github.com/rhpds/rhpds.litemaas
- Upstream: https://github.com/rh-aiservices-bu/litemaas

## ⚠️ Migration Notice (June 21, 2026)

The old cluster (litellm-rhpds namespace) is being decommissioned on June 21, 2026.
- Labs provisioned via demo.redhat.com automatically use the new cluster — no action needed.
- Self-service API key holders must create new keys on the new cluster before June 21.
- New portal: https://maas-rhdp-frontend.apps.maas.redhatworkshops.io

## Production Endpoints

- User Portal: https://maas-rhdp-frontend.apps.maas.redhatworkshops.io
- AI Proxy (OpenAI-compatible): https://maas-rhdp.apps.maas.redhatworkshops.io/v1
- Public URL (via Akamai, coming soon): https://maas.demo.redhat.com

## Available Models (as of June 2026)

All models are visible in the portal. Models above 120B parameters require admin approval to subscribe.

### Vertex AI (via rh-summit-ai-workshops GCP project)

- minimax-m2 — MiniMax M2 reasoning model, RESTRICTED (>120B, admin approval required)
- qwen3-235b — Alibaba Qwen 3 235B, RESTRICTED (>120B, admin approval required)
- gpt-oss-120b — GPT OSS 120B, Chat, open access
- gpt-oss-20b — GPT OSS 20B, Chat, open access

### Locally Hosted (llm-hosting namespace, Intel Gaudi / GPU / CPU)

- granite-3-2-8b-instruct — IBM Granite 3.2 8B, Chat
- granite-4-0-h-tiny — IBM Granite 4.0 H Tiny, Chat, tool calling
- granite-2b-cpu — IBM Granite 2B (CPU), Chat
- llama-scout-17b — Meta Llama 4 Scout 17B, Chat, 400K context
- llama-31-70b-cpu — Meta Llama 3.1 70B (CPU), Chat
- Llama-Guard-3-1B — Meta Llama Guard 3 1B, Safety classification
- codellama-7b-instruct — Meta CodeLlama 7B, Code chat
- deepseek-r1-distill-qwen-14b — DeepSeek R1 Distill Qwen 14B, Reasoning
- qwen3-14b — Alibaba Qwen 3 14B, Chat, multilingual
- qwen25-3b-cpu — Alibaba Qwen 2.5 3B (CPU), Chat
- microsoft-phi-4 — Microsoft Phi-4, Chat, compact
- phi3-mini-cpu — Microsoft Phi-3 Mini (CPU), Chat
- nomic-embed-text-v1-5 — Nomic Embed Text, Embeddings
- Docling — Document conversion (endpoint: /docling/v1/convert/source)

## Model Access Policy

All models require admin approval to subscribe via the self-service portal. Models above 120B parameters (minimax-m2, qwen3-235b) have additional scrutiny — choose models appropriate to your use case, not just for size. This is an internal RHDP service; do not use for customer-facing production workloads.

## Ansible Deployment

The rhpds.litemaas Ansible collection deploys all components:
Namespace -> PostgreSQL 16 -> Redis 7 -> LiteLLM Proxy (5 replicas) -> LiteMaaS Backend (1 replica) -> LiteMaaS Frontend (3 replicas) -> Routes + OAuthClient -> NetworkPolicies -> Key Cleanup CronJob

Key variables: ocp4_workload_litemaas_namespace, ocp4_workload_litemaas_oauth_enabled, ocp4_workload_litemaas_ha_litellm_replicas, ocp4_workload_litemaas_ha_backend_replicas, ocp4_workload_litemaas_ha_frontend_replicas

## Claude Code MCP Integration

The LiteMaaS MCP server (docs/day2/mcp.html) runs on OpenShift and lets Claude Code query and manage RHDP MaaS in plain English.

**Available tools:** list_models, get_model_health, list_users, get_spend_summary, get_daily_stats, get_pod_status, update_user_budget, list_virtual_keys

**MCP server endpoint:** https://litemaas-mcp.apps.<your-cluster>/mcp
**Transport:** MCP 2025-03-26 Streamable HTTP
**Deployment:** opt-in via ocp4_workload_litemaas_deploy_mcp=true

## User Role Hierarchy

- admin — Full platform control (add to litemaas-admins OCP group, then user logs in via frontend)
- adminReadonly — Read-only access to all admin views
- user — Browse models, request access, manage own API keys (auto-assigned on first OAuth login)

## Load Balancing

LiteLLM has a built-in router that activates automatically when two or more model entries share the same model_name. No explicit router configuration is required.

**Health check:**
```
GET /health?model=<model_name>
```

**Router settings:**
```
GET /router/settings
```

Reference: https://rhpds.github.io/rhpds.litemaas/docs/day2/model-routing.html#load-balancing
